data_in.description: File path to mzML file (uncompressed, standard gzip, or indexed gzip format)
File path to SQLite database file containing spectra table
Encoding parameter for text file handling (e.g., UTF-8)

data_out.description: File handler instance (IndexedGzip, StandardGzip, StandardMzml, or SQLiteDatabase) correctly instantiated for the input file type
Test report documenting correct dispatch to each of the four handler classes with passing assertions

domain_knowledge: Indexed gzip files are compressed mzML files with embedded random-access indices that allow seeking without full decompression, whereas standard gzip requires sequential reading.
The `__getitem__` method enables random access to spectra by ID; the `read` method enables sequential iteration, and both must be implemented by custom file handler wrappers.
SQLiteDatabase wrapper must instantiate a sqlite3 connection and implement cursor queries to retrieve spectrum XML elements by ID, then parse them back into Spectrum or Chromatogram objects.
File extension-based dispatch (.gz, .db, default .mzML) is the primary routing mechanism in `_open`, with optional content inspection for gzip type determination.
The encoding parameter (typically UTF-8) must be passed consistently to all file handler constructors to ensure correct XML parsing across different file types.

Confirm that the dispatch mechanism is robust to edge cases (empty files, corrupted headers, missing extensions) and fails gracefully
Review whether the handler class resolution prioritizes file extension or content inspection in a manner consistent with pymzML design philosophy

expected_outputs: File handler instance (IndexedGzip, StandardGzip, StandardMzml, or SQLiteDatabase) correctly instantiated for the input file type
Test report documenting correct dispatch to each of the four handler classes with passing assertions
finding: The _open method implements conditional dispatch by first checking if the path ends with '.gz' and then inspecting whether it is an indexed gzip file; if indexed it instantiates IndexedGzip, otherwise StandardGzip; for '.db' extensions it instantiates SQLiteDatabase; for all other paths it defaults to StandardMzml.

File path to SQLite database file containing spectra table
Encoding parameter for text file handling (e.g., UTF-8)
methodology_summary: Define `_open` method with path and encoding parameters.
Implement conditional dispatch: inspect file extension (.gz, .db, or default) and call optional indexed-gzip check for .gz files.
Instantiate correct handler class (IndexedGzip, StandardGzip, StandardMzml, or SQLiteDatabase) with path and encoding arguments.
Return the instantiated handler object to the caller.

Validation: Unit test each dispatch branch with representative file paths (uncompressed mzML, standard .mzML.gz, indexed .mzML.gz, .db) and assert handler type is correct; integration test by passing handler to Reader and verifying spectrum retrieval succeeds.

task_description: Implement the `_open` method in `FileInterface` to conditionally dispatch file handling to `IndexedGzip`, `StandardGzip`, `StandardMzml`, or `SQLiteDatabase` based on file extension, and verify each dispatch branch correctly instantiates the appropriate handler class.

task_inputs.label: File path to mzML or SQLite database file
Character encoding for text file handling

task_objective: Implement the FileInterface._open method with conditional logic to dispatch to the correct file handler class based on file extension and indexed gzip detection.

task_outputs.label: Instantiated file handler object (IndexedGzip, StandardGzip, StandardMzml, or SQLiteDatabase)
Test report verifying correct handler instantiation for each file type

title: Reconstruct the FileInterface dispatch logic for routing mzML file types to their respective handler classes
uncertainty_notes: This card was generated by the LLM-assisted pipeline and needs scientific expert review.
Each TracedClaim's evidence_span has been substring-checked against its source section; see logs/llm_calls.jsonl and capsules/<task_id>/quality_report.json for groundedness results.
workflow_description: 1. Define the `_open` method signature in `FileInterface` to accept a file path parameter. 2. Implement conditional logic to inspect file extension: if path ends with '.gz', check indexed gzip status and dispatch to `IndexedGzip` or `StandardGzip` accordingly; if path ends with '.db', instantiate `SQLiteDatabase`; otherwise, instantiate `StandardMzml`. 3. For each conditional branch, instantiate the appropriate file handler class with the path and encoding parameters. 4. Unit test each dispatch branch by calling `_open` with mock files of each type (.mzML, .mzML.gz with index, .mzML.gz without index, .db) and verify the returned handler is an instance of the expected class. 5. Integration test by passing dispatched handlers to downstream Reader operations to confirm correct spectrum retrieval.
